km.Syleham, Suffolk, England: សីលេហាម, សូហ្វូលក, ប្រទេសអង់គ្លេស

zh.Syleham, Suffolk, England: 英國素福爾可希勒哈姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Watson Clark於1451年出生在英國素福爾可希勒哈姆時，他的父親Lewis Clark26歲，母親Mary Hollister25歲。他於1470年與Sarah Jane Decker在英國素福爾可希勒哈姆結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1470年之後在英國。

zh-hans.Syleham, Suffolk, England: 英国素福爾可希勒哈姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Watson Clark于1451年出生在英国素福爾可希勒哈姆时，他的父亲Lewis Clark26岁，母亲Mary Hollister25岁。他于1470年与Sarah Jane Decker在英国素福爾可希勒哈姆结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1470年之后在英国。

nameMeanings.name: Clark
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

variants.name: Clarke
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clerk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Jacques.
English: variant of   Clark  .
Americanized form of French   Clerc   and perhaps also of the Dutch and Flemish cognates   De Klerk   and   De Clerck   or of variants of these names. Compare   Clark   3.

variants.name: Clerkin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Liam, Brendan, Dympna, Seamus.

Irish (Monaghan): from an Irish pet form of English clerk ‘cleric’ (Irish cléireach), see Clark . Alternatively, an Anglicized form of Ó Cléireacháin, ‘descendant of little cleric’, an Irish diminutive of the word. See also McClurkin , an Antrim surname from Mac Cléirchín ‘son of little cleric’.

variants.name: Calarco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Carmelo, Fausto, Nunziato, Salvatore.
Italian (southern): probably a nickname derived from Greek kalē archē ‘good beginning’. Compare Clark 4.

variants.name: Clerc
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alain, Odette, Sebastien, Serge.

French: occupational or status name for a member of a minor religious order or for a scholar, Old French clerc, from Late Latin clericus, from Greek klērikos, a derivative of klēros ‘inheritance, legacy’, with reference to the priestly tribe of Levites (see Levy ) ‘whose inheritance was the Lord’. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established. In the Middle Ages it was virtually only members of religious orders who learned to read and write, so that the term clerc later came to denote any literate man, a scholar (hence its modern meaning ‘scribe, secretary’ or ‘clerk’). Compare Leclerc .

variants.name: Clarkson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): patronymic from   Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eudard

variants.name: Eduardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard
meanings: form of English Edward .

nameMeanings.name: Watson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish, English, and Irish: patronymic meaning ‘son of Wat’, a pet form of Walter that was particularly common in Scotland and northern England. See Watt .

variants.name: Walter
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Watson Clark was born in 1451, in Syleham, Suffolk, England, his father, Lewis Clark, was 26 and his mother, Mary Hollister, was 25. He married Sarah Jane Decker in 1470, in Syleham, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died after 1470, in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Watson Clark est né en 1451, à Syleham, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Lewis Clark, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Mary Hollister, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Sarah Jane Decker en 1470, à Syleham, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé après 1470, à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Watson Clark 1451 in Syleham, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lewis Clark 26 und seine Mutter Mary Hollister 25. Er hat 1470 in Syleham, Suffolk, England Sarah Jane Decker geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb nach 1470 in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Watson Clark è nato il nel 1451 nel Syleham, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, Lewis Clark, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Mary Hollister, aveva 25 anni. Lui sposato Sarah Jane Decker nel 1470, nel Syleham, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì dopo il 1470, nel England.

ja.Syleham, Suffolk, England: イギリススフォルクシレハム
ja.lifeSummary: Edward Watson Clarkが1451年にイギリススフォルクシレハムで生まれた時，父Lewis Clarkは26歳，母Mary Hollisterは25歳であった。1470年にイギリススフォルクシレハムでSarah Jane Deckerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1470年以後にイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Watson Clark(이)가 1451년 영국 수폴크 실함에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lewis Clark(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Hollister(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1470년 영국 수폴크 실함에서 Sarah Jane Decker(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1470년 이후 영국에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Watson Clark nasceu em 1451, em Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Lewis Clark, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Mary Hollister, tinha 25 anos. Ele casou-se com Sarah Jane Decker em 1470, em Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu após 1470, em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Watson Clark родился в 1451 г. в местности силэам, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Lewis Clark было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Hollister, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Sarah Jane Decker в 1470 г. в местности силэам, суффолк, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер после 1470 г. в местности Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edward Watson Clark nació en 1451, en Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Lewis Clark, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Mary Hollister, tenía 25 años. Se casó con Sarah Jane Decker en 1470, en Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió después de 1470, en Inglaterra.
